item.title: About
item.description: Wilbur Watts M.Ed. Candidate, Mathematics Education · Penn State University Welcome to my portfolio. I'm Wilbur Watts — a Penn State master's student dedicated to making rigorous math education accessible to every learner, whether they're in a college classroom or a workforce training program. This space reflects my journey as both a practitioner and a scholar: the curricula I've designed, the pedagogical questions I'm exploring, and my ongoing commitment to meeting students exactly where ...

item.title: Specialization
item.description: Area of Specialization My expertise lies at the intersection of equity-centered mathematics curriculum design and the study of mathematical identity among post-secondary and adult learners. What unifies these two threads is a single animating question: how do the structures we build into mathematics instruction — the problems we choose, the sequences we design, the norms we establish — either invite students into mathematical thinking or quietly signal that they don't belong there? I work...

item.title: Philosophy
item.description: Teaching Philosophy My teaching practice is grounded in a blend of classroom experience, curriculum design, and ongoing scholarly inquiry. As a mathematics instructor within both college courses and workforce development programs, I have taught and supported learners across a wide range of mathematical levels—from developmental arithmetic to calculus. In my roles as a Supplemental Instruction leader, tutor, and curriculum developer, I have designed instructional materials, facilitated colla...

item.title: Publications
item.description: Ladson-Billings, G. (2021). Culturally relevant pedagogy 2.0: a.k.a. the remix. Harvard Educational Review, 91(1), 1–25. https://doi.org/10.17763/1943-5045-91.1.1 Boaler, J. (2017). Mathematical mindsets: Unleashing students’ potential through creative math, inspiring messages, and innovative teaching. Jossey-Bass.Paris, D., & Alim, H. S. (2017). Culturally sustaining pedagogies: Teaching and learning for justice in a changing world. Teachers College Press. Skovsmose, O. (2011). An invita...

item.title: Competency
item.description: My professional expertise spans three interconnected domains: equity-centered mathematics curriculum design, post-secondary and workforce mathematics instruction, and the study of mathematical identity development among non-traditional learners. These areas reflect both the practical knowledge I have built through years of direct instruction and tutoring, and the scholarly grounding I have developed through graduate study at Penn State University. Curriculum Vitae Areas of ExpertiseEquity-Cen...

item.title: Domain 1
item.description: Equity-Centered Curriculum Design My work in mathematics education is anchored in the belief that curriculum is not neutral—it communicates who belongs, who is expected to succeed, and whose ways of thinking are valued. As both an instructor and curriculum designer in college and workforce settings, I have focused on developing instructional materials that center access alongside rigor. My experience designing curricula for adult learners, many of whom return to mathematics after years of d...

item.title: Domain 2
item.description: Instructional Practice & Student Engagement My instructional practice is shaped by extensive experience teaching and supporting students across college mathematics courses and workforce education programs. In these environments, I have worked with learners whose mathematical backgrounds vary widely, requiring flexible and responsive teaching strategies. My goal as an instructor is to create a classroom environment where students are active participants in meaning-making rather than passive re...
